preregistration: this file, committed before any counting. Owner ask (17-Jul, in-session, quoted): 'another experiment to answer whether the changes to cut ceremony actually worked'.
hypothesis: The two ceremony-cut events — ADR 0062 (14-Jul: /decide panel re-scoped to a lite default) and commit 860c865 (16-Jul: the every-PR /retrospect mandate retired to on-demand) — changed (a) ceremony volume and (b) the instruments' useful output. Direction unstated for (b): cutting a mandate could equally starve the retrospective channel or concentrate it. A null or an adverse finding is recorded the same as a favorable one.
design: OBSERVATIONAL, zero generation spend — counts over the repo's own record (PR bodies, ADR corpus, docs/pdca/session-log.txt, git log). No arms, no judges; the verdict is descriptive counts plus an honest reading, never a KEEP/CUT.

windows.panels: ADRs 0043-0061 (pre-0062) vs 0063-0074 (post-0062), by Panel: line — the cut event is ADR 0062 itself
windows.retrospectives: PRs created 2026-07-10..2026-07-16T00:00Z (pre-860c865, mandate era) vs PRs created after 860c865 (16-Jul) through 17-Jul (on-demand era; includes open PRs — merge state recorded)

metrics: M1 panel rate: fraction of ADRs whose Panel: line records spawned advisors (vs none/owner-direct/gates-only), pre vs post 0062
M2 retrospective rate: fraction of PRs with a run retrospective (PR body Retrospective section or a branch retrospective commit), pre vs post 860c865
M3 retrospective yield: adopted findings per RUN retrospective (a finding counts as adopted when a commit/ADR/PR-body line lands it; counted from PR bodies and commits, each cited), pre vs post
M4 spawn-log volume: docs/pdca/session-log.txt lines per day, pre vs post 16-Jul (caveat: under-logs raw-agent panels, over-logs builtin verify; ADR 0072 discounts pre-fix two-dot lines)
disclosures: ANALYST CONTAMINATION: the analyst (this session) executed most of the post-cut window's work and has read the history before pre-registering. Mitigation: every metric is a count over committed text, cited per item, reproducible by anyone from the same commands; no judged scores.
CONFOUNDS: windows differ in work mix (pre: measurement campaigns; post: fixes + benchmarks), the post window is ~1.5 days, and n is small. The verdict is exploratory by construction.
The post window's PRs are mostly unmerged; counts read their pushed branches and bodies as-is.
kill_condition: if either window yields fewer than 4 PRs or 5 ADRs, record INSUFFICIENT-N for that metric rather than a reading.
